title: Harden snapshot verification integrity and migrations
summary: Makes repository-defined verification tamper-evident, adds explicit asynchronous suite execution and strict snapshot aggregation, surfaces trust and policy caveats in the UI, and preserves legacy configuration, snapshots, and run evidence through version-aware readers.
userVisibleChange: Snapshot readers can distinguish EVE-executed evidence from agent-reported or legacy claims and can see incomplete, failed, stale, or policy-weakened verification without misleading green states.

decisions.title: Keep v1 tamper-evident rather than claiming tamper resistance
decisions.rationale: Repository-local policy and evidence remain writable by an actor with shell and Git access, so the product discloses that boundary explicitly.

decisions.title: Resolve required suites from repository policy and Git context
decisions.rationale: Callers may opt into stricter suites but cannot supply commands or de-escalate the configured profile.

decisions.title: Read legacy formats without rewriting them
decisions.rationale: Config v1/v2, snapshot 0.1/schema-less records, and original compact-digest run evidence retain their historical semantics while unknown versions fail closed.

validation.output: Command package tests passed under the race detector.
8 test files and 21 tests passed; TypeScript and Vite production build succeeded.
7 installer tests passed and the package dry-run completed.
